intent: Set up a safer web-research workflow (exa/tavily/firecrawl, research subagent, sandbox and keyring hardening) starting from a narrow skills-overview question.
finding: Classic scope-escalation (manic-mode signature) within a single 13-hour-span session on 2026-04-19. The user opened at 09:36 with 'are there skills for searching/research, are they hooked in?' and by 22:47 had grown the scope through: adopt exa+firecrawl → drop firecrawl → build a sandboxed web-research subagent → enforce it programmatically → dev-container lifecycle → GH keyring + secret scanning on all private repos → domain allowlist for the container → wrap agent outputs as untrusted → measure three research speed tiers → pool of pre-spun subagents → 'honeypots for agents that leak env vars'. Each sub-goal is reasonable but the trajectory is pure 'and also X'. Scope was still escalating to a brand-new research direction at 22:47. No explicit frustration or fatigue language. Severity tension: scope-escalation is a core manic-mode signal but this is one session on one day with no late-night (after 23:00) work, so keeping at medium rather than high.
existing_mitigation: Wellbeing nudges exist (~/.claude/wellbeing/work-hours-nudge.py, habit-nudge.py, sunset-walk-nudge.py) fired from UserPromptSubmit, but they are time-of-day nudges — they do not detect mid-session scope escalation or suggest a save-point when a session has been widening for hours.

intent: Juggle several concurrent work threads on the same day (autodoro timer bugfix, Starguard security scan, general sandboxing/web-research setup, git-push-from-sandbox).
finding: Seven user-driven sessions opened on 2026-04-19 across three distinct projects, with several running concurrently (afc7c920 12:13-19:24 overlapping with b6aa1d47 09:36-22:47 and the autodoro sessions). 'Multiple sessions within a few hours' is one of the catalogued manic-mode signatures. Hours-of-day distribution is healthy (all within 08:00-23:00 local, no late-night), which keeps this low rather than medium. Worth noting as a single data point; would only rise in severity if the pattern recurs across consecutive days or starts pushing past 23:00.
existing_mitigation: work-hours-nudge.py enforces start/stop-of-day boundaries but does not count concurrent open sessions or surface a 'you have N threads live' signal.

intent: Resume a long-running autodoro debugging thread across multiple days (sessions af013e0d spans 2026-04-16 to 2026-04-19, with a frustrated 'ANSWER MY DIRECT WUESTION' moment on 2026-04-18).
finding: The autodoro session af013e0d was resumed three times (2026-04-16, 04-18, 04-19) to debug the same underlying timer/recording issue. On 2026-04-18 the user shouted in all caps ('ANSWER MY DIRECT WUESTION: ...Doesn't this give enough info to fix the issue?') — an explicit frustration signal while still within normal hours (16:52 local). Not a wellbeing alarm on its own (frustration is a productivity signal), but the combination of (a) same-bug-dragging-on-for-days and (b) escalating frustration tone is the kind of drag that pushes into burnout if it recurs. Flagging as low to keep on the radar for next run's pattern check.
existing_mitigation: None specific to 'same bug across multiple days' drag. The frustration itself belongs to productivity (Claude needed rescue on autodoro), but the persistence-over-days aspect is wellbeing-shaped.
